title: Billions Must Philosophize
body: Strauss and Aristotle agree that to be human is to philosophical, and to be philosophical is to be political. There is a notion in our world today that says an individual can "mind their own business" without caring about "politics" (whatever they mean by that) and avoid engaging in them. At the same time, they make alliances with coworkers, create pacts family and friends, and use leverage at work to get more days off. We are political animals (Aristotle). There might have been a time some 50 years ago when a corporate job could pay a mortgage, support a large family, and the family man could just keep his head down and go to-and-from the office without making so much as a peep outside of his concentric circles of influence. However, those days are gone. To be anything other than a peon in the 2020's means distinguishing yourself, risking comfort for failure, and speaking your mind.
